subtaskTitle: Study the hadith on the Prophet's wudu water usage (Bukhari & Muslim)

payload.sentence:
Note the hadith where the Prophet saw Sa`d performing wudu and said, "What is this extravagance?" — Sa`d asked, "Is there extravagance in wudu?" and the Prophet replied, "Yes, even if you are at a flowing river."
5.

subtaskTitle: Study the hadith on the Prophet's wudu water usage (Bukhari & Muslim)
Teach family members the Sunnah method and its environmental significance
Read and reflect on Quran 7:31, 17:26-27, and 25:67 on moderation and waste

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) told of a man who earned Allah's forgiveness for giving water to a thirsty dog (Bukhari and Muslim), and he rebuked a companion who had taken chicks from a nest, commanding him to return them (Abu Dawud).

subtaskTitle: Study the hadith collections on animal rights (Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud)
Ensure any pets or animals in your care are properly fed, sheltered, and treated with kindness

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever keeps a pet and does not feed it or water it, Allah will hold him accountable on the Day of Judgement" (Muslim).

subtaskTitle: Report any cases of animal cruelty you witness to appropriate authorities

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Whoever among you sees a wrong, let him change it with his hand; if he cannot, then with his tongue; if he cannot, then with his heart — and that is the weakest of faith" (Muslim).

subtaskTitle: Teach children the Islamic ethic of rahma (mercy) toward all creatures

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Allah shows mercy to those who show mercy to His creation" (Abu Dawud).

subtaskTitle: Plant the tree or plant in your garden, balcony, or a community space

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "There is no Muslim who plants a tree or sows a seed, and then a bird, or a person, or an animal eats from it, except that it is regarded as a charitable gift (sadaqah) for him" (Bukhari and Muslim).

subtaskTitle: Water and care for it regularly — set a reminder if needed

Avoid purchasing products made from endangered species or illegal wildlife trade
Register and attend at least one event this season

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said that the most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small (Bukhari).

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Each of you is a shepherd, and each of you is responsible for his flock" (Bukhari & Muslim).

subtaskTitle: Identify three product categories where you can apply the tayyib standard more rigorously

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Take on only as much as you can do of good deeds, for the best of deeds is that which is done consistently, even if it is small" (Ibn Majah).

subtaskTitle: Build relationships with farmers — ask about their growing practices

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "The buyer and the seller have the option of cancelling or confirming the deal as long as they have not parted" (Bukhari) — commerce was conducted face-to-face, with trust built through repeated honest interaction.

subtaskTitle: Donate or sell excess clothing responsibly

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself" (Bukhari).

subtaskTitle: Connect with existing circular economy networks, incubators, or Islamic finance providers

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "The believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts enforce each other" (Bukhari).

subtaskTitle: Set up a simple ordering and distribution system (shared spreadsheet, collection point)

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Make things easy and do not make them difficult" (Bukhari).

subtaskTitle: Share with your masjid, Islamic centre, and community social media channels

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "When a person dies, their deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for them" (Muslim).
